text:
ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
Tertiary Academic Medical Center

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF BIRTH: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ORDERING PROVIDER: Urologic Oncology Service
DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD, FCAP

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

SPECIMEN IDENTIFIER

Accession Number: [REDACTED]
Specimen Designation: S-[REDACTED]
Specimen Type: Core needle biopsy вЂ” right renal mass (CT-guided, interventional radiology)
Number of Cores Submitted: Three (3) cores received in formalin
Clinical Indication: Large right renal mass, 14.2 cm, incidentally found on imaging workup; pre-operative tissue diagnosis requested prior to surgical planning by the urologic oncology and multidisciplinary tumor board.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

CLINICAL HISTORY

The patient is a 63-year-old female of Nigerian-American descent with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus and hypertension. She presents with a large (14.2 cm) heterogeneous right renal mass with macroscopic tumor thrombus extending into the right renal vein (grade 2 Mayo venous thrombus), a right retroperitoneal lymph node measuring 2.4 cm at the renal hilum, and bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 1.8 cm, right lower lobe). CT-guided core needle biopsy of the primary right renal mass was performed by Interventional Radiology for tissue diagnosis to guide surgical and multidisciplinary oncologic planning.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

GROSS PATHOLOGY

Received in formalin are three (3) tan-white to tan-yellow cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments ranging from 1.2 to 1.6 cm in length and 1 to 2 mm in maximum diameter. The cores are variably firm; two cores have areas of yellow-orange discoloration with focal glistening cut surfaces, consistent with lipid-rich neoplastic tissue. One core exhibits a gray-white, firm, fibrous region occupying approximately 20-25% of its length. No grossly identifiable hemorrhage or fresh necrosis in the submitted biopsy fragments, though one core has a focal dark red area at its tip. All three cores are entirely submitted in a single cassette labeled "right renal mass biopsy."

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

All three cores are evaluable and show adequate tumor tissue for diagnosis. The overall architecture is well-sampled.

Clear Cell Component (approximately 80% of sampled tissue):
The predominant component consists of nests and alveolar clusters of polygonal cells with abundant optically clear to lightly eosinophilic cytoplasm. The cytoplasmic clarity is due to dissolution of intracytoplasmic glycogen and lipid during standard processing. Cells are arranged in compact acinar nests and sheets separated by a delicate, arborizing network of thin-walled, sinusoidal blood vessels вЂ” the classic "chicken-wire" vascular pattern characteristic of clear cell renal cell carcinoma. Nuclei are markedly enlarged and pleomorphic with prominent macronucleoli readily visible at low-power magnification (Fuhrman/ISUP nuclear grade 4 features). Prominent nucleoli are present in virtually all tumor cells, measuring greater than the diameter of a red blood cell on 10x objective. Intranuclear pseudoinclusions are identified focally. Scattered mitotic figures are present throughout, including atypical tripolar forms. Focal geographic areas of coagulative tumor necrosis are present within the clear cell component. Hemorrhage within the vascular spaces and intratumoral hemorrhage are also noted.

Sarcomatoid Component (approximately 20% of sampled tissue):
In approximately 20% of the submitted biopsy material вЂ” predominantly within one core вЂ” there is an abrupt transition from the conventional clear cell architecture to a high-grade spindle cell neoplasm with sarcomatoid morphology. This zone is composed of interlacing fascicles and storiform arrays of markedly atypical spindled cells with scant cytoplasm, irregular hyperchromatic nuclei, and conspicuous nucleoli. The mitotic rate in the sarcomatoid component is markedly elevated, with up to 8 mitotic figures per 10 high-power fields, including atypical forms. Focal geographic necrosis is prominent in the sarcomatoid areas, with necrotic foci abutting viable sarcomatoid tumor. No convincing rhabdoid differentiation is identified (no cells with eccentric eosinophilic cytoplasmic inclusions or vesicular nuclei with prominent nucleoli in a rhabdoid pattern are seen on careful review of all three cores).

Additional Microscopic Findings:
Lymphocytic infiltrates, predominantly CD3+ T cells by morphology, are present at the interface between the clear cell and sarcomatoid zones. No granulomatous inflammation. No spindle cell lesions morphologically resembling dedifferentiated liposarcoma or synovial sarcoma are seen. No heterologous elements (osteosarcomatous, chondrosarcomatous, or rhabdomyosarcomatous differentiation) are identified.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS

RIGHT RENAL MASS, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY (3 CORES):

   CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA
   вЂ” Fuhrman Nuclear Grade: 4 (ISUP/WHO Grade 4)
   вЂ” With sarcomatoid differentiation comprising approximately 20% of sampled tumor volume
   вЂ” No rhabdoid differentiation identified
   вЂ” Geographic necrosis present
   вЂ” See Ancillary Studies below

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

ANCILLARY STUDIES

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC)
Performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue sections using standard avidin-biotin complex (ABC) technique with appropriate positive and negative controls:

   Carbonic Anhydrase IX (CA9 / CAIX):
      Result: POSITIVE вЂ” diffuse, strong membranous staining in greater than 95% of clear cell tumor cells; membranous staining also retained, albeit focally, in sarcomatoid component
      Interpretation: Strongly supportive of clear cell RCC; CA9 expression driven by HIF-1alpha activation downstream of VHL loss

   PAX8:
      Result: POSITIVE вЂ” diffuse nuclear staining in clear cell component; retained (weak to moderate) in sarcomatoid component
      Interpretation: Confirms renal/Mullerian epithelial lineage; supports renal primary origin of both components

   RCC Antigen (gp200):
      Result: POSITIVE вЂ” apical/luminal membranous staining pattern in clear cell component
      Interpretation: Consistent with renal cell carcinoma of clear cell type

   CD10:
      Result: POSITIVE вЂ” membranous staining in clear cell component
      Interpretation: Consistent with proximal tubular differentiation; supportive of ccRCC

   Vimentin:
      Result: POSITIVE вЂ” diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in sarcomatoid component; focal, weak staining in clear cell component
      Interpretation: Expected mesenchymal marker positivity in sarcomatoid differentiation

   Cytokeratin 8/18 (CK8/18):
      Result: FOCAL POSITIVE вЂ” positive in approximately 30-40% of clear cell component; largely negative in sarcomatoid component
      Interpretation: Focal keratin expression typical for ccRCC; loss of keratin expression in sarcomatoid component is well-recognized and does not alter the diagnosis

   TFE3:
      Result: NEGATIVE
      Interpretation: Excludes TFE3-rearranged translocation RCC (Xp11 translocation RCC), an important diagnostic consideration in clear cell-like RCC

   TFEB:
      Result: NEGATIVE
      Interpretation: Excludes TFEB-amplified/rearranged RCC

   Cathepsin K:
      Result: NEGATIVE (focal, non-specific background only)
      Interpretation: Further supports exclusion of translocation-associated RCC

   SOX10:
      Result: NEGATIVE
      Interpretation: Excludes melanocytic/nerve sheath lineage in the spindle cell component

   Desmin:
      Result: NEGATIVE
      Interpretation: No convincing myogenic differentiation in the sarcomatoid component

   S100:
      Result: NEGATIVE
      Interpretation: Excludes spindle cell melanoma and nerve sheath tumor

   INI1 (SMARCB1):
      Result: RETAINED nuclear expression (normal)
      Interpretation: No loss of INI1 expression; excludes rhabdoid tumor and SMARCB1-deficient RCC

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Companion Diagnostic Assay):
   Platform/Clone: Ventana PD-L1 SP263 / Dako 22C3 pharmDx equivalent
   Scoring Method: Combined Positive Score (CPS)
   CPS Calculation: Staining evaluated on viable tumor cells, tumor-associated lymphocytes, and macrophages within the biopsy
   Result: CPS = 12
   Interpretation: CPS в‰Ґ 1 considered PD-L1 positive. CPS 12 indicates moderate PD-L1 expression; both the tumor microenvironment immune cells and a subset of tumor cells contribute to this score. Sarcomatoid component shows stromal immune cell PD-L1 positivity predominantly.

KI-67 PROLIFERATIVE INDEX:
   Method: MIB-1 antibody immunohistochemistry with digital image analysis
   Clear Cell Component: Ki-67 proliferative index approximately 12% (range 10-15% across sampled areas)
   Sarcomatoid Component: Ki-67 proliferative index approximately 35% (range 30-40% across sampled areas)
   Interpretation: Markedly elevated Ki-67 in the sarcomatoid component is consistent with high-grade aggressive biology. The difference in proliferative rates between the two components further underscores the clonal evolution and dedifferentiation in the sarcomatoid zone.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

DIAGNOSTIC COMMENT / PATHOLOGIST'S NOTE

The morphologic and immunophenotypic features are diagnostic of clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) with high-grade sarcomatoid dedifferentiation. The immunoprofile вЂ” strong, diffuse CAIX positivity; PAX8 positivity; CD10 positivity; TFE3 and TFEB negativity вЂ” is characteristic of ccRCC arising from the proximal renal tubular epithelium with biallelic VHL inactivation (the expected molecular event, consistent with the radiologic and clinical presentation).

The sarcomatoid component (approximately 20% of sampled tissue) represents a form of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition and high-grade dedifferentiation that is well-recognized in RCC (occurring in approximately 5% of all RCC cases). Sarcomatoid differentiation in RCC is not a separate histologic entity but rather a dedifferentiation pattern that may arise in any RCC subtype, most commonly ccRCC. Its presence is associated with a significantly worse prognosis and with enhanced responsiveness to immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy in prospective clinical trials (notably, in the CheckMate 214 ipilimumab/nivolumab trial, the sarcomatoid subgroup derived particular benefit from combination immunotherapy versus sunitinib monotherapy).

The PD-L1 CPS of 12 (moderate expression) is consistent with the immunologically active tumor microenvironment typical of sarcomatoid RCC.

This biopsy is diagnostic and is consistent with the imaging findings of a large, heterogeneous right renal mass with necrosis and vascular thrombus. Correlation with clinical, radiologic, and molecular data is recommended in the multidisciplinary context.

Correlation with comprehensive molecular/genomic profiling (NGS) is noted to be pending/ordered as per urologic oncology request.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

SIGN-OUT

Diagnosing Pathologist: [REDACTED], MD, FCAP
Department of Pathology, Surgical Pathology Division
Case reviewed and signed out electronically.

Quality Assurance: Case reviewed by attending pathologist with genitourinary subspecialty expertise. IHC controls verified. Diagnosis concordant with clinical and radiologic impression.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
END OF REPORT
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
